title: Mahāmṛtyuñjaya Mantra Japa
century: Vedic (Ṛgveda, c. 1500 BCE; Taittirīya Saṃhitā, c. 1200 BCE)
description: The "great death-conquering" mantra (Tryambakam) addressed to Tryambaka ("the three-eyed one," i.e. Śiva-Rudra), attested in the Vedic Saṃhitās. Moderate caution — protective Vedic recitation; traditionally performed under a qualified Vedic pandit for specific remedial purposes. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.
